messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0074",
  "title": "五条规则，一个目标：让用户留下来、提升付费率、持续提升全链路增长。用户留存的「黄金五法则」",
  "page_index": 45,
  "page_range": "45"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0075",
    "title": "留存从第0天开始，而不是第30天",
    "page_range": "45",
    "summary": "**Topics**: 用户留存策略、新手引导设计、用户生命周期管理、增长策略有效性评估、ROI优化与增长循环。\n**Entities**: 留存（Retention）、第0天/第30天、新手引导流程、首次付费、用户生命周期管理、创意体系、ROI、用户忠诚度、增长循环、营销链路。\n**Dates/Timing**: 留存计算起始节点：明确主张从第0天（用户接触即刻）开始，而非第30天。\n**Definitions**: 留存并非营销链路中的孤立环节或单一阶段，而是一套持续运转的效果系统；是判断整体增长策略是否有效的关键标志。\n**Procedures**: 将用户参与机制嵌入新手引导流程；在首次付费发生前清晰传递核心价值；推动用户生命周期管理与创意体系协同运作。\n**Claims**: 高价值用户从初始阶段即建立强连接，而非仅“留下来”；成功留存的用户是ROI的放大器，可提升忠诚度并持续驱动下一轮增长；留存质量直接决定整体增长策略的有效性。\n**Metrics**: ROI（作为留存与体系协同后的放大结果）；时间基准对比（第0天 vs 第30天）。\n**Tables/Figures**: 本节未包含。\n**Evidence Types**: 本节为策略性论述与原则性主张，未提供数据、案例或实证研究。\n**Retrieval Questions Answered**: 留存应从何时开始计算？如何设计新手引导以提升早期用户参与？留存与ROI、用户忠诚度及增长循环的关联是什么？如何在产品/营销体系中定义留存的角色？生命周期管理与创意体系如何协同以优化留存？"
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0076",
    "title": "衡量关系深度，而非触达广度",
    "page_range": "45",
    "summary": "This section answers retrieval questions regarding:\n- **Topics & Claims:** Why measuring relationship depth should be prioritized over reach breadth, and why tracking quality supersedes quantity in user engagement strategies.\n- **Metrics:** Which specific indicators are recommended for evaluation: interaction frequency, single-session depth, and reactivation cost.\n- **Definitions/Concepts:** How the combination of these three metrics forms a comprehensive (360°) understanding of the user base, implicitly defining \"relationship depth\" as a quality-driven, multi-dimensional engagement model.\n- **Entities:** User base.\nThe excerpt does not contain dates, step-by-step procedures, tables, figures, or specific empirical evidence types."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0077",
    "title": "在用户产生疲劳之前完成内容更新",
    "page_range": "45",
    "summary": "This section provides retrieval-ready information to answer questions about:\n• **Topics:** Creative fatigue mitigation, content update scheduling, ad performance optimization, and narrative consistency in growth cycles.\n• **Entities:** Aarki (Creative Intelligence platform), CTR (Click-Through Rate).\n• **Timeframes/Exposure Counts:** Creative relevance decays within a few days; performance degradation occurs after 4 ad impressions.\n• **Key Concepts:** Creative relevance decay, structured refresh cycles, user attention loss/fatigue.\n• **Procedures/Recommendations:** Proactively plan update rhythms, execute content refreshes before attention declines, and maintain consistent storytelling throughout the growth cycle.\n• **Claims:** Unrefreshed creatives rapidly lose relevance and engagement; structured update cycles prevent performance degradation and stabilize campaign results.\n• **Metrics:** ~45% CTR drop after 4 exposures without refresh; ~30% improvement in performance stability with structured refresh cycles.\n• **Evidence Types:** Quantitative platform analytics (Aarki Creative Intelligence data), cited with a reference marker.\n• **Tables/Figures:** None included in this section."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0078",
    "title": "再营销，本质上是研发(R&D)",
    "page_range": "45",
    "summary": "This section answers retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Definition/Topic:** How is remarketing fundamentally characterized? It is defined as a form of R&D, where each campaign functions as a controlled test.\n- **Procedure/Strategy:** How should remarketing insights be operationalized? By identifying value propositions that effectively engage at-risk/churning users and reverse-applying these insights to user acquisition workflows to improve overall efficiency.\n- **Claim/Metric:** How does remarketing performance compare to new user acquisition? Remarketing users yield a significantly higher Lifetime Value (LTV) than new users acquired during the same period.\n- **Entity/Evidence Type:** What source supports this performance claim? Observational findings from the company Aarki.\n- **Dates/Tables/Figures:** None are mentioned or included in this section."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0079",
    "title": "放大有效策略，暂停进入平台期的动作",
    "page_range": "45",
    "summary": "- **可回答主题**：营销策略优化、预算分配原则、长期价值与短期速赢的权衡、再营销（Retargeting）预算稳定性管理。\n- **涉及实体**：Avi Das（Aarki 首席营收官）、Aarki（公司）。\n- **相关日期**：2025年1月（数据来源标注：202501）。\n- **核心概念/定义**：“平台期动作”（指效果停滞、需暂停的策略）、“短期势能”与“可持续动能”、“长期价值用户群体与创意体系”。\n- **操作建议/流程**：放大已验证有效的策略；暂停进入平台期的动作；避免追逐短期势能，转向维持可持续动能；将预算持续投入能构建长期价值的用户群与创意体系，而非追求短期速赢。\n- **核心主张/结论**：长期保持稳定再营销预算的团队，通常能获得更平滑的ROI曲线与更低的投放波动性。\n- **涉及指标**：ROI曲线平滑度、投放波动性（campaign volatility）。\n- **证据类型**：企业高管观点/专家建议（Aarki CRO Avi Das 引述）；企业内部数据（Aarki internal data, 202501）。"
  }
]

message.content:
**Topics**: User retention strategy, onboarding design, user lifecycle management, growth strategy evaluation, ROI optimization, relationship depth vs. reach breadth, creative fatigue mitigation, content update scheduling, ad performance optimization, remarketing as R&D, marketing strategy optimization, budget allocation, and long-term value vs. short-term wins.
**Entities**: Retention, Day 0/Day 30, onboarding flow, first payment, creative system, ROI, user loyalty, growth loop, marketing funnel, user base, Aarki (Creative Intelligence platform & company), CTR, Avi Das (Aarki CRO).
**Dates/Timing**: Retention calculation baseline: Day 0 (immediate user contact) vs. Day 30; creative relevance decay timeframe: within days, with performance degradation after 4 ad impressions; data source timestamp: January 2025 (202501).
**Definitions**: Retention is defined as a continuous effect system and a core indicator of overall growth strategy effectiveness, not an isolated marketing stage. "Relationship depth" is a quality-driven, multi-dimensional engagement model. Remarketing is fundamentally characterized as R&D, where each campaign functions as a controlled test. "Plateau actions" refer to strategies with stagnant performance that should be paused.
**Procedures**: Embed engagement mechanisms into onboarding; communicate core value before first payment; align lifecycle management with creative systems; proactively plan content update rhythms and refresh creatives before attention declines; maintain consistent storytelling across growth cycles; identify value propositions for at-risk/churning users and reverse-apply insights to acquisition workflows; scale proven strategies, pause plateauing actions, and allocate budget toward long-value user groups and creative systems rather than chasing short-term wins.
**Claims**: High-value users establish strong connections early; successful retention amplifies ROI, boosts loyalty, and drives continuous growth; retention quality directly dictates growth strategy effectiveness; measuring relationship depth supersedes tracking reach breadth; unrefreshed creatives rapidly lose relevance, while structured update cycles prevent degradation and stabilize results; remarketing yields significantly higher LTV than new user acquisition in the same period; teams maintaining stable retargeting budgets achieve smoother ROI curves and lower campaign volatility.
**Metrics**: ROI (as an amplification outcome), time baseline comparison (Day 0 vs. Day 30), interaction frequency, single-session depth, reactivation cost, ~45% CTR drop after 4 exposures without refresh, ~30% improvement in performance stability with structured refresh cycles, Lifetime Value (LTV) comparison (remarketing vs. new users), ROI curve smoothness, campaign volatility.
**Evidence Types**: Strategic/principled assertions (no empirical data for the first rule); quantitative platform analytics from Aarki Creative Intelligence; observational findings from Aarki; executive/expert insights from Aarki CRO Avi Das; internal company data (Jan 2025).
**Tables/Figures**: None included across the subsections.

response_text:
**Topics**: User retention strategy, onboarding design, user lifecycle management, growth strategy evaluation, ROI optimization, relationship depth vs. reach breadth, creative fatigue mitigation, content update scheduling, ad performance optimization, remarketing as R&D, marketing strategy optimization, budget allocation, and long-term value vs. short-term wins.
**Entities**: Retention, Day 0/Day 30, onboarding flow, first payment, creative system, ROI, user loyalty, growth loop, marketing funnel, user base, Aarki (Creative Intelligence platform & company), CTR, Avi Das (Aarki CRO).
**Dates/Timing**: Retention calculation baseline: Day 0 (immediate user contact) vs. Day 30; creative relevance decay timeframe: within days, with performance degradation after 4 ad impressions; data source timestamp: January 2025 (202501).
**Definitions**: Retention is defined as a continuous effect system and a core indicator of overall growth strategy effectiveness, not an isolated marketing stage. "Relationship depth" is a quality-driven, multi-dimensional engagement model. Remarketing is fundamentally characterized as R&D, where each campaign functions as a controlled test. "Plateau actions" refer to strategies with stagnant performance that should be paused.
**Procedures**: Embed engagement mechanisms into onboarding; communicate core value before first payment; align lifecycle management with creative systems; proactively plan content update rhythms and refresh creatives before attention declines; maintain consistent storytelling across growth cycles; identify value propositions for at-risk/churning users and reverse-apply insights to acquisition workflows; scale proven strategies, pause plateauing actions, and allocate budget toward long-value user groups and creative systems rather than chasing short-term wins.
**Claims**: High-value users establish strong connections early; successful retention amplifies ROI, boosts loyalty, and drives continuous growth; retention quality directly dictates growth strategy effectiveness; measuring relationship depth supersedes tracking reach breadth; unrefreshed creatives rapidly lose relevance, while structured update cycles prevent degradation and stabilize results; remarketing yields significantly higher LTV than new user acquisition in the same period; teams maintaining stable retargeting budgets achieve smoother ROI curves and lower campaign volatility.
**Metrics**: ROI (as an amplification outcome), time baseline comparison (Day 0 vs. Day 30), interaction frequency, single-session depth, reactivation cost, ~45% CTR drop after 4 exposures without refresh, ~30% improvement in performance stability with structured refresh cycles, Lifetime Value (LTV) comparison (remarketing vs. new users), ROI curve smoothness, campaign volatility.
**Evidence Types**: Strategic/principled assertions (no empirical data for the first rule); quantitative platform analytics from Aarki Creative Intelligence; observational findings from Aarki; executive/expert insights from Aarki CRO Avi Das; internal company data (Jan 2025).
**Tables/Figures**: None included across the subsections.
